# MCP Conformance Tests - Transports
# Tests for transport mechanisms: stdio and HTTP

# ============================
# STDIO TRANSPORT TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 1: STDIO Basic Functionality
# Start the test server with stdio transport
[stdio] exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio
[stdio] cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Initialize the connection
[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
[stdio] exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
[stdio] stdout '"result":'

[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
[stdio] exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Test basic request-response
[stdio] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"stdio test"}}
[stdio] exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
[stdio] stdout '"result":'
[stdio] stdout '"stdio test"'

# TEST 2: STDIO Message Encoding
# Messages must be UTF-8 encoded
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"UTF-8 test: 你好, привет, こんにちは, 안녕하세요"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"UTF-8 test: 你好, привет, こんにちは, 안녕하세요"'

# TEST 3: STDIO Message Delimiters
# Messages must be delimited by newlines
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":4,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"line 1"}}\n{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":5,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"line 2"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"id":4'
stdout '"line 1"'
stdout '"id":5'
stdout '"line 2"'

# TEST 4: STDIO Output Validation
# Server must not write anything to stdout that is not a valid MCP message
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio --validate-stdout
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Initialize and make a request
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":6,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":7,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"validate stdout"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'
! stdout 'Invalid MCP message'

# TEST 5: STDIO Error Logging
# Server may write to stderr for logging
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio --verbose
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"
cmp stderr "Verbose logging enabled"

# Initialize and make a request
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":8,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":9,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"test stderr logging"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'
cmp stderr "Received request: echo"

# ============================
# HTTP TRANSPORT TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 6: HTTP Basic Functionality
# Start the test server with HTTP transport
[http] exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:8765
[http] cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:8765"

# Initialize the connection via HTTP
[http] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":10,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
[http] exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8765
[http] stdout '"result":'

[http] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
[http] exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8765
# No response expected for notifications

# Test basic request-response over HTTP
[http] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":11,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"http test"}}
[http] exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8765
[http] stdout '"result":'
[http] stdout '"http test"'

# TEST 7: HTTP Headers
# Client must include Accept header with application/json
exec mcp-spy --http=http://localhost:8765 --capture-headers
cmp stderr "MCP spy listening on http://localhost:8765"

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":12,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"header test"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8765
stdout '"result":'
cmp stderr "Received headers: Accept: application/json, text/event-stream"

# TEST 8: HTTP Response Codes
# Responses containing only JSON-RPC notifications should return 202 Accepted
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/test"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8765 --show-status-code
stdout "Status: 202 Accepted"

# Responses for JSON-RPC requests should return 200 OK
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":13,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"status test"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8765 --show-status-code
stdout "Status: 200 OK"

# TEST 9: Server-Sent Events (SSE)
# Start server with SSE support
[sse] exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:8766 --sse
[sse] cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:8766 with SSE enabled"

# Initialize the connection
[sse] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":14,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
[sse] exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8766
[sse] stdout '"result":'

[sse] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
[sse] exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8766

# Open an SSE stream to receive server messages
[sse] exec mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8766 --sse-listen &

# Send a request to trigger SSE messages
[sse] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":15,"method":"progress_echo","params":{"message":"sse test","progress_updates":3}}
[sse] exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8766

# Verify we receive progress notifications and final result via SSE
[sse] stdout 'event: message'
[sse] stdout 'data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/progress"'
[sse] stdout 'data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":15,"result":'
[sse] stdout '"sse test"'

# TEST 10: HTTP Session Management
# Start server with session support
exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:8767 --session
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:8767 with session support"

# Initialize to get a session ID
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":16,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"TransportTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8767 --capture-headers
stdout '"result":'
cmp stderr "Response headers: Mcp-Session-Id:"
# Extract session ID from response headers
session_id=$(grep "Mcp-Session-Id:" stderr | awk '{print $3}')

# Use session ID in subsequent requests
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8767 --header="Mcp-Session-Id: $session_id"

# Missing session ID should result in 400 Bad Request
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":17,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"session test"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8767
stdout "Status: 400 Bad Request"

# Including session ID should work
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":18,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"session test"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8767 --header="Mcp-Session-Id: $session_id"
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"session test"'

# TEST 11: Multiple Connections
# Server should handle multiple concurrent connections
exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:8768 --sse
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:8768 with SSE enabled"

# Initialize first connection
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":19,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"Connection1","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8768
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8768

# Open first SSE stream
exec mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8768 --sse-listen --client-id=client1 &

# Initialize second connection
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":20,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"Connection2","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8768
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8768

# Open second SSE stream
exec mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8768 --sse-listen --client-id=client2 &

# Each message should only be delivered to one stream
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":21,"method":"broadcast_to_one","params":{"message":"connection test"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --http=http://localhost:8768
stdout '"result":'

# Each client should receive different messages
stdout 'client1: Received message'
stdout 'client2: Received message'
# But they should not receive the same message (no broadcasting)
! stdout 'client1 and client2: Received the same message'
